title: The Ruined Terminal
story: You wake in the ruins of Data City. A cracked terminal flickers. The System cannot speak until output is restored.
challenge_text: Restore the terminal so the system can say: System Online
clues: Fragment of the Volume of Output: The system once used the word 'print' to send messages.
Fragment: Messages were wrapped inside quotation marks "like this".
Fragment: The command placed the message inside parentheses ( ).

title: The Supply Cache
story: A supply machine sputters to life. It needs memory to store how many rations remain.
challenge_text: Store the number 5 in a variable named rations and print it.
clues: Fragment of the Volume of Variables: Values were stored using the = symbol.
Fragment: A name like rations can hold a number.
Fragment: Stored values could be printed just like messages.

title: The Water Pump
story: A district water pump requires a repeating signal to prime the pipes.
clues: Fragment of the Volume of Loops: The word 'for' began repetition.
Fragment: Loops end with a colon :

title: The Firewall Gate
story: You reach the old Firewall Gate. It only opens for safe travelers. The control panel still works, but it needs decision logic restored.
challenge_text: Write Python code that creates a variable named access with the value True. If access is True, print Access Granted. Otherwise print Access Denied.

title: The Memory Lift
story: Deep beneath the city, the Memory Lift is still intact. It can raise the first Archive Core, but only if you restore a reusable system function.
challenge_text: Write Python code that defines a function named restore() which prints Archive Restored, then call the function.
